profiles.wizard_title: Build a browser profile through a clear step-by-step wizard
profiles.wizard_body: Start with the Firefox behavior people will notice first, then open fine tuning only where you need it. Export stays on the last step.
profiles.wizard_context_new: You are creating a new profile. Set the essentials first, then jump to any step you need.
profiles.wizard_context_existing: You are editing an existing profile. Open any step you need, save when ready, and export from the last step.
profiles.wizard_context_cloned: You are adjusting a draft based on {name}. Keep the useful parts, then change only the areas this variant needs.
profiles.wizard_step_one_copy: Profile name, Firefox channel, scenario, starting preset, and CIS layer.
profiles.wizard_step_two_copy: Network access, browser behavior, home surfaces, search, and navigation defaults.
profiles.wizard_step_three_copy: Privacy controls, permissions, cookies, cleanup, and hardening posture.
profiles.wizard_security_map_body: Start with the shared posture, then review cleanup, site access, and built-in protection only where this profile needs exceptions.
profiles.wizard_step_four_copy: Mozilla accounts, language, translation, add-ons, bookmarks, and website handling.
profiles.wizard_step_five_copy: Built-in AI surfaces, visual search, and smart browser features.
profiles.wizard_step_six_copy: Check the setup in plain language, then save and download the version you want.
profiles.wizard_shell_body: Use this technical view when you need settings that are not yet available in the simpler guided form.
profiles.wizard_review_empty_attention: Nothing needs attention here right now.
profiles.wizard_review_empty_settings: No items outside Guided editor are listed here right now.
profiles.wizard_settings_covered_body: Open this map only when you want to jump to a specific Firefox Settings area.
profiles.wizard_preferences_covered_body: Open this map when you need the lower-level preference controls for this area.
profiles.wizard_preferences_general_hand: Open this only when the guided browser basics above are not enough and you need lower-level preference keys.
profiles.wizard_preferences_search_hando: Open this only when the guided search and suggestion controls above are not enough and you need lower-level preference keys.
profiles.wizard_shell_recommended_body: High-value settings that already have a guided control in this step.
profiles.wizard_shell_additional_body: Settings that belong to this area, but still work better in All settings for now.
profiles.wizard_shell_preferences_body: Preference sections that still sit next to this step until they get a simpler guided form.
profiles.wizard_shell_raw_body: Settings we keep in All settings until a simpler form exists.
profiles.wizard_shell_coverage_empty: This step does not currently expose schema-tracked policy coverage in guided mode.
profiles.wizard_shell_coverage_full: Guided editor already covers all {mapped} schema-tracked policy areas for this step.
profiles.wizard_shell_coverage_partial: Guided editor covers {mapped} of {total} schema-tracked policy areas here. The remaining {advanced} stay available in All settings.
profiles.wizard_guided_coverage_empty: This step currently focuses on the main guided choices only. Use All settings if you need lower-level policy coverage here.
profiles.wizard_guided_coverage_full: Everything schema-tracked for this step is already available in Guided editor.
profiles.wizard_guided_coverage_partial: Most common settings are available here in Guided editor. {advanced} of {total} schema-tracked policy areas still stay in All settings.
profiles.wizard_guided_coverage_examples: Still only in All settings: {items}, and {remaining} more.
profiles.wizard_guided_coverage_open: Open All settings for this step
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_recommended: No recommended policies are mapped into this step yet.
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_additional: No additional mapped policies are listed for this step yet.
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_preferences: No preference sections are attached to this step.
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_raw: This step currently does not need a raw JSON fallback bucket.
profiles.wizard_shell_inline_partial_not: Some nested fields are still preserved through raw JSON.
profiles.wizard_shell_true_map_hint: One hostname or origin per line.
profiles.wizard_shell_json_hint: Use valid JSON for nested lists or objects.
profiles.wizard_shell_json_error: Nested values must be valid JSON.
profiles.wizard_shell_number_error: Value must be a valid number.
profiles.wizard_shell_nested_unsupported: Some nested fields still fall back to raw JSON.
profiles.wizard_step_two_index_body: Jump to the part you need instead of scanning every browser-default control.
profiles.wizard_step_four_index_body: Jump to default search, managed engines, suggestions, or the local review without scanning the whole step.
profiles.wizard_user_environment_map_bod: Jump to the part of the user environment you need instead of scanning every operational control.
profiles.wizard_browser_defaults_map_bod: Jump between browser basics, access, startup, search, and review without scanning the whole step.
profiles.wizard_browser_defaults_review_: Check network access, startup surfaces, and search defaults together before moving on.
profiles.wizard_profile_identity_body: Name the profile and choose the Firefox schema channel before you decide how its managed baseline should begin.
profiles.wizard_starter_body: Choose the job this profile needs to do. The wizard will apply the closest starting baseline, and you can still replace it explicitly when needed.
profiles.wizard_scenarios_body: Pick one scenario first. It selects the matching baseline automatically so the first decision stays focused.
profiles.wizard_scenario_corporate_body: Best when most people need one stable corporate Firefox profile with the usual enterprise guardrails.
profiles.wizard_scenario_shared_body: Best for labs, kiosks, training rooms, and any device that rotates between multiple people.
profiles.wizard_scenario_hardened_body: Best when stronger security defaults, tighter browser surfaces, and stricter shutdown behavior matter most.
profiles.wizard_scenario_addons_body: Best when the main job is to roll out, lock down, or fine-tune extensions without starting from a blank profile.
profiles.wizard_scenario_targeted_body: Best when you mostly want to keep the current baseline or build only a narrow set of changes manually.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_body: Choose a scenario to see which baseline we recommend and what that path emphasizes first.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_corpora: Start from {baseline} for the fastest broad corporate rollout.
Gives you a balanced managed Firefox baseline without starting from scratch.
Keeps network, homepage, privacy, and add-on defaults aligned for ordinary employee devices.
Best when you expect only light follow-up tweaks after the initial baseline.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_shared: Start from {baseline} when the browser lives on a shared or tightly guided device.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_shared_: Biases the flow toward permissions, site restrictions, and fewer escape paths.
Makes the privacy and website steps more important right after setup.
Best when one device serves many people or needs a kiosk-style posture.
profiles.wizard_shared_device_workflow_b: Use this checklist when one Firefox profile rotates between many people or should behave more like a kiosk than a personal browser.
profiles.wizard_shared_device_workflow_a: This profile is currently aimed at a shared-device or kiosk-style posture. Finish the decisions below so startup, site access, and session cleanup reinforce each other.
profiles.wizard_shared_device_workflow_h: A shared-device startup choice is already configured.
Choose where the browser should land first and whether that starting point should stay locked.
Set this only if the shared device should always land on the same managed page.
profiles.wizard_shared_device_workflow_s: Site access rules are already configured for this profile.
Decide whether this device should block open browsing, allow only specific sites, or tighten site permissions.
Use this if the shared device should restrict which sites or permissions are available.
profiles.wizard_shared_device_workflow_p: Private browsing is already managed for this profile.
Decide whether people should be able to open private windows on this shared device.
Leave this alone unless the shared device should block private browsing explicitly.
profiles.wizard_shared_device_workflow_c: Shutdown cleanup is already configured for this profile.
Choose whether browser traces should be cleared whenever the session ends.
Use this when browser traces should not survive between people or sessions.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_hardene: Start from {baseline} when stronger browser hardening matters more than comfort defaults.
Pushes stricter defaults for shutdown cleanup, lockdown, and safer browser surfaces.
Makes the privacy and network decisions the next high-value checkpoints.
Best for higher-risk environments that expect tighter guardrails from the start.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_addons: Start from {baseline} when extension policy is the main rollout goal.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_addons_: Keeps a solid managed base in place so add-on rules do not float on an empty profile.
Makes step 4 the main follow-up area for install rules, curated profiles, and lock state.
Best when the profile mostly exists to deliver or constrain browser extensions.
profiles.wizard_scenario_summary_targete: Start from {baseline} to keep the current saved profile as your working base.
Preserves the current profile so you can make narrower edits instead of resetting everything.
Works well for audits, point fixes, and small policy additions.
Best when you already have a live baseline and only need a focused change.
Start from {baseline} when you only need a narrow custom profile without a full preset.
Leaves the draft mostly blank so you can add only the rules you actually need.
Works well for proof-of-concept profiles or one-off policy experiments.
Best when a big baseline would create more cleanup than value.
profiles.wizard_setup_impact_body: One compact view of the selected scenario, baseline impact, follow-up workflow, and preview.
profiles.wizard_baseline_recommended_bod: Open this only when the scenario is right but the automatic baseline is not the one you want.
profiles.wizard_baseline_secondary_body: Use these when you want to keep an existing profile untouched or build everything manually.
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_body: Select a baseline to preview the main managed areas it will set up for you.
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_body: Hover or focus a scenario or baseline to see which managed areas it would change in the current draft.
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_selecte: Applying {label} would change these areas first.
Applying {label} with {cis} would change these areas first.
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_no_majo: Applying {label} would stay close to the current draft.
Applying {label} with {cis} would stay close to the current draft.
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_same: No major managed baseline changes stand out from the current draft.
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_homepag: Homepage and startup behavior would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_proxy: Proxy routing and browser network path would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_network: Enterprise trust and access rules would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_privacy: Privacy and telemetry defaults would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_feature: Account, language, or browser guidance rules would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_addons: Managed add-on rollout rules would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_sites: Website, permission, or shared-device restrictions would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_preview_hardeni: Browser hardening and shutdown cleanup would change
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_blank: Blank draft starts without a managed baseline.
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_blank_i: No starter policies are applied yet.
Pick settings step by step in the guided flow.
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_keep: Keep current leaves the existing profile baseline in place.
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_keep_it: Current saved profile rules stay as your starting point.
Best when you only need targeted edits instead of a reset.
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_addons: Managed add-on controls and tighter install rules
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_shared_: Shared-device restrictions for sites and permissions
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_hardeni: Extra browser hardening and shutdown cleanup
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_selecte: {label} will be merged with {cis} before fine-tuning.
profiles.wizard_baseline_summary_cis_rev: CIS conflicts staying on the baseline value for manual review: {count}
profiles.wizard_cis_body: Optionally merge CIS Firefox ESR controls on top of the selected baseline. Level 2 includes Level 1.
profiles.wizard_cis_none_copy: Use only the selected product baseline and tune it in the next steps.
profiles.wizard_cis_l1_copy: Adds baseline CIS hardening while keeping core enterprise usability.
profiles.wizard_cis_l2_copy: Applies Level 1 and the stricter Level 2 controls for hardened environments.
profiles.wizard_cis_exceptions_body: Capture why you kept or adjusted a base setting when CIS suggested a stricter value.
profiles.wizard_cis_exceptions_empty: No CIS exceptions need notes right now.
profiles.wizard_cis_exceptions_count_emp: No CIS exceptions need notes right now.
profiles.wizard_cis_exceptions_note_plac: Explain why this override is acceptable and when it should be revisited.
profiles.wizard_cis_final_summary_body: Compact outcome counts for the selected CIS overlay. Detailed reasons stay in the focused review below.
profiles.wizard_cis_final_summary_empty: No CIS overlay is selected for this profile.
profiles.wizard_starter_blank_copy: Use a clean document and turn on policies yourself step by step.
profiles.wizard_starter_keep_copy: Best for editing an existing profile without replacing its current baseline.
profiles.wizard_starter_basic_copy: Applies a fuller managed workstation baseline with enterprise trust, home, messaging, and extension controls.
profiles.wizard_starter_basic_note: Best default for employee laptops and standard managed workstations.
profiles.wizard_starter_classroom_copy: Locks shared devices down further with site allowlisting, permission gates, and fewer escape paths.
profiles.wizard_starter_classroom_note: Best for labs, kiosks, training rooms, and devices that rotate between people.
profiles.wizard_starter_soc_copy: Adds stricter network, privacy, shutdown, and browser hardening defaults for higher-trust environments.
profiles.wizard_starter_soc_note: Best for higher-risk environments where tighter browser guardrails are expected.
profiles.wizard_group_privacy_copy: Control telemetry, Firefox account surfaces, private windows, and password prompts.
profiles.wizard_group_privacy_hint: Start with the browser-level privacy defaults people will feel every day.
profiles.wizard_hardening_body: Choose the overall hardening level first, then fine-tune privacy, lockdown, and site controls below only where this profile needs exceptions.
profiles.wizard_hardening_hint: This is the fastest path for a hardened profile. Use the sections below when only part of the posture needs to change.
profiles.wizard_hardening_preset_default: Leave privacy and lockdown behavior at the normal Firefox baseline.
profiles.wizard_hardening_preset_balance: Reduce data collection, tighten high-risk browser surfaces, and enable practical HTTPS-only protection.
profiles.wizard_hardening_preset_strict_: Tighten risky surfaces harder, disable more sensitive features, and add shutdown cleanup.
profiles.wizard_hardening_section_state_: Firefox is using its usual security posture until you choose a hardened baseline or tune individual privacy controls below.
Custom privacy and hardening controls are active
profiles.wizard_hardening_impact_telemet: Balanced and Strict reduce browser data collection first.
profiles.wizard_hardening_impact_https_c: Balanced enables practical HTTPS-only protection; Strict locks down more browser surfaces.
profiles.wizard_hardening_impact_private: Strict also limits private browsing and saved-login prompts.
profiles.wizard_hardening_impact_cleanup: Strict includes cleanup for key local traces at shutdown.
profiles.wizard_hardening_impact_site_da: Site data stays editable as a separate exception area below.
profiles.wizard_cleanup_body: Decide whether Firefox should clear local browsing traces whenever the browser closes on managed devices.
profiles.wizard_cleanup_hint: This is most useful on shared or higher-risk devices where browser traces should not survive between sessions.
profiles.wizard_cleanup_preset_defaults_: Leave shutdown cleanup at the normal Firefox baseline.
profiles.wizard_cleanup_preset_shared_co: Clear the main browsing traces on shutdown without locking every cleanup choice down.
profiles.wizard_cleanup_preset_strict_co: Clear broader browser traces on shutdown and lock the cleanup posture in place.
profiles.wizard_cleanup_section_state_em: Firefox is keeping its usual shutdown behavior here until you choose a cleanup posture.
profiles.wizard_cleanup_section_state_sh: Main browsing traces will be cleared on shutdown
profiles.wizard_cleanup_section_state_st: Strict locked shutdown cleanup is active
profiles.wizard_cleanup_section_state_cu: Custom shutdown cleanup rules are active
profiles.wizard_hardening_governance_bod: Use the recommendation below to confirm the main hardening posture, shutdown cleanup, and whether deeper privacy exceptions still need a technical pass.
profiles.wizard_hardening_governance_act: This profile already carries hardening decisions. Use the recommendation below to confirm that the shared posture, cleanup, and deeper privacy rules still line up.
profiles.wizard_hardening_governance_han: Open deeper hardening rules only when this profile needs lower-level privacy branches or technical cleanup exceptions beyond the common guided posture.
profiles.wizard_hardening_governance_ite: Choose the main hardening posture first so Firefox has a clear baseline for privacy and risky browser surfaces.
Main hardening posture is already set to {value}.
Choose a shutdown cleanup posture when traces should not survive between people or sessions.
Site permissions and cookies still use their usual behavior. That is fine unless this profile needs stricter site controls too.
Managed site and cookie areas already configured: {count}.
No deeper hardening exceptions yet. That is fine unless this profile needs lower-level privacy branches or technical cleanup rules.
Lower-level hardening signals or cleanup branches already configured: {count}.
profiles.wizard_privacy_preset_defaults_: Leave privacy, private windows, and password prompts at their normal managed defaults.
profiles.wizard_privacy_preset_signals_c: Turn off telemetry and studies while leaving the rest of day-to-day browsing closer to normal.
profiles.wizard_privacy_preset_strict_co: Reduce data collection and remove private windows and saved-login prompts on more locked-down devices.
profiles.wizard_privacy_fine_tuning_body: Open fine tuning when this profile needs a custom mix of privacy and password rules instead of one of the common levels above.
profiles.wizard_privacy_outcome_telemetr: Browser data collection and Firefox studies live together here.
profiles.wizard_privacy_outcome_password: Private Browsing access, saved-login prompts, and password manager behavior stay in one outcome group.
profiles.wizard_group_lockdown_copy: Reduce advanced configuration paths and tighten risky browser capabilities.
profiles.wizard_group_lockdown_hint: Then decide which advanced browser surfaces should stay unavailable.
profiles.wizard_lockdown_preset_defaults: Leave advanced browser surfaces in their normal state.
profiles.wizard_lockdown_preset_balanced: Block obvious risky surfaces and turn on HTTPS-Only Mode without pushing every control to the maximum.
profiles.wizard_lockdown_preset_strict_c: Block advanced entry points more aggressively and force stronger HTTPS behavior for hardened environments.
profiles.wizard_lockdown_fine_tuning_bod: Open fine tuning when this browser needs a more specific lockdown mix than the common levels above.
profiles.wizard_privacy_outcome_lockdown: Configuration pages, developer tools, PDF handling, and HTTPS-only controls are grouped by browser surface risk.
profiles.wizard_privacy_site_controls_bo: Keep the most important site permission and cookie rules in the guided flow instead of burying them in technical editing.
profiles.wizard_privacy_site_controls_hi: Start by deciding how strict Firefox should be with site permissions and cookies.
profiles.wizard_site_data_preset_default: Leave site permissions and cookie behavior at their usual managed defaults.
profiles.wizard_site_data_preset_balance: Block new permission prompts in the main categories and tighten third-party cookies without fully locking everything down.
profiles.wizard_site_data_preset_strict_: Lock the most sensitive site permissions and apply a stronger cookie posture for tightly managed devices.
profiles.wizard_site_data_fine_tuning_bo: Open fine tuning when this profile needs custom site exceptions, permission categories, or cookie rules beyond the common levels above.
profiles.wizard_privacy_outcome_site_dat: Site permissions and cookie behavior stay together because both define what websites can remember or access.
profiles.wizard_group_updates_copy: Tune update policy, default-browser prompts, search suggestions, and download behavior.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_body: Keep the core day-to-day browser behavior aligned with what users see in Firefox Settings -> General.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_focus_hin: Start from the browser task that matters here: update flow, default-browser prompts, download prompts, or a managed combination of those controls.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_preset_de: Leave ordinary update checks and browser prompts untouched for now.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_preset_up: Use this when the main task is managing Firefox or system add-on update behavior.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_preset_br: Use this when the main task is suppressing the make-default-browser prompt.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_preset_do: Use this when download prompts should ask for a save location each time.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_preset_ma: Use this when the profile needs a coordinated mix of update, prompt, and download controls.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_section_s: Firefox will keep its usual update checks, default-browser prompts, and download prompt behavior here. Change this when the browser should feel more managed day to day.
profiles.wizard_upkeep_governance_body: Keep update behavior, browser prompts, and download handling aligned with how managed this browser should feel day to day.
profiles.wizard_upkeep_governance_active: This profile already has a managed upkeep posture. Check that updates, prompts, and download handling still match the workstation policy you want.
profiles.wizard_upkeep_governance_item_u: Update-related controls are already managed here: {count}.
Pick how Firefox and system add-on updates should behave on this workstation.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if update cadence should stay standard.
profiles.wizard_upkeep_governance_item_p: The make-default-browser prompt is already being managed here.
Decide whether this workstation should keep or suppress the default-browser prompt.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if users can decide on their own.
profiles.wizard_upkeep_governance_item_d: The browser already asks where to save downloads each time.
Decide whether people should confirm the download location each time.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if download flow does not need extra guidance.
profiles.wizard_upkeep_governance_item_m: This profile already uses a coordinated mix of upkeep rules beyond the main quick choices.
Optional: open the deeper controls if this workstation needs a stricter mix of update and browser-behavior rules.
You can come back here later if this workstation needs a more coordinated upkeep posture.
profiles.wizard_home_surfaces_workflow_b: Use this checklist when the browser should start in a managed way, land on specific pages, or show a more curated Firefox Home experience.
profiles.wizard_home_surfaces_workflow_a: This profile already carries some managed home or startup choices. Check that homepage, landing surfaces, and Firefox Home cards still match the browser experience you want people to see.
profiles.wizard_home_surfaces_workflow_i: A managed homepage or startup target is already configured here.
Decide whether the browser should open an internal portal, restore sessions, or lock people onto a specific managed start page.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if the browser can start normally.
New tab behavior or first-run/update landing pages are already being managed here.
Choose whether new-tab behavior or first-run/update pages should be redirected into a managed landing flow.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if Firefox can keep its normal new-tab and onboarding surfaces.
Firefox Home cards are already configured beyond the default card mix.
Pick whether Firefox Home should emphasize search, shortcuts, or a tighter card mix for this browser.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if Firefox Home does not need a managed card mix.
This profile already combines homepage, landing overrides, and Firefox Home changes into one coordinated experience.
Optional: tighten the landing flow further if startup pages and Firefox Home should work together as one managed experience.
Come back here later if this browser needs a more coordinated startup and landing experience.
profiles.wizard_sync_body: Decide whether people can sign in to a Mozilla account and whether Firefox should show managed account messaging.
profiles.wizard_sync_focus_hint: Start from the part of the account experience you actually need to control: sign-in access, browser guidance, or both together.
profiles.wizard_sync_focus_defaults_copy: Leave Mozilla account access and Firefox guidance at their usual browser behavior.
profiles.wizard_sync_focus_accounts_copy: Use this when the main task is allowing or blocking Mozilla account sign-in.
profiles.wizard_sync_focus_guidance_copy: Use this when the profile mainly needs to manage onboarding, prompts, or recommendation surfaces.
profiles.wizard_sync_focus_managed_copy: Use this when the profile needs both managed sign-in behavior and managed in-browser guidance.
profiles.wizard_sync_fine_tuning_body: Open fine tuning when you need to shape sign-in, onboarding, or in-browser messages.
profiles.wizard_sync_section_state_empty: Firefox will keep its usual Mozilla account sign-in and onboarding behavior until you change it here.
profiles.wizard_schema_user_messaging_in: Turn the browser messages people should or should not see on first, then lock the result if needed.
profiles.wizard_schema_user_messaging_gr: Control the first-run experience and whether users can change these messaging surfaces later.
Choose whether Firefox can recommend add-ons, features, or other Mozilla content.
Use these controls for address-bar interventions and Firefox Labs style entry points.
profiles.wizard_language_translation_bod: Set browser language and website translations in this step, then move to the AI step only if you want Firefox to expose smarter browser features.
profiles.wizard_language_focus_hint: Start from the language job you need to solve. Then open the matching card to set the exact locales or translation rule.
profiles.wizard_language_preset_defaults: Leave browser locales and website translations at normal Firefox behavior.
profiles.wizard_language_preset_locales_: Use this when the profile should request specific browser languages.
profiles.wizard_language_preset_translat: Use this when website translations should be disabled explicitly.
profiles.wizard_language_preset_managed_: Use this when the profile should both request specific locales and manage translation behavior.
profiles.wizard_language_section_state_e: Firefox will keep its usual language and website translation behavior until you choose what should change in this step.
profiles.wizard_language_governance_body: Use this checklist when browser languages, website translations, or adjacent AI-language surfaces should follow a more deliberate managed posture.
profiles.wizard_language_governance_acti: This profile already has some managed language decisions. Check that requested locales, translation posture, and adjacent language-facing surfaces still match the browsing environment you want.
profiles.wizard_language_governance_item: Requested browser locales are already configured here: {count}.
Decide whether this browser should request specific locales instead of following the normal browser language mix.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if browser locales can follow the normal device or user setup.
Website translations are already explicitly managed in this step.
Website translations are already explicitly turned off in this step.
Choose whether website translations should stay available or be turned off deliberately for this profile.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if website translations do not need central control.
This profile already combines requested locales and managed translation behavior into one coordinated language baseline.
Optional: review whether browser locales and translation behavior should be governed together as one language baseline.
Come back here later if this profile needs a more coordinated language baseline.
profiles.wizard_language_ai_handoff_skip: Language and translation are already covered here. You can skip the AI step unless this profile also needs built-in AI or visual search rules.
profiles.wizard_language_ai_handoff_go_a: This profile already touches AI-adjacent browser behavior. Open the AI step too if you want those controls to stay intentional.
profiles.wizard_language_ai_handoff_opti: Only go to the AI step if this profile should manage built-in AI features or smart browser surfaces.
profiles.wizard_ai_body: Manage the AI-related Firefox 151 controls exposed by the current template, from feature-by-feature availability to adjacent smart browser surfaces.
profiles.wizard_ai_map_body: Start with the posture, then review feature availability and adjacent smart browser surfaces where this profile needs them.
profiles.wizard_ai_esr_title: This schema does not support AI settings
profiles.wizard_ai_esr_body: Firefox ESR 140.11 does not publish supported AI policy fields, so there is nothing to configure on this step.
profiles.wizard_ai_esr_state: This schema does not support AI settings.
profiles.wizard_ai_policy_unavailable: This Firefox schema does not include this AI policy. Switch to a schema that supports AI settings to manage it here.
profiles.wizard_ai_posture_body: Choose a baseline posture, then adjust the exported policy controls below when you need feature-by-feature behavior.
profiles.wizard_ai_posture_defaults_copy: Keep Firefox AI behavior at its usual defaults.
profiles.wizard_ai_posture_disable_copy: Use this when built-in AI features should be turned off deliberately.
profiles.wizard_ai_posture_availability_: Keep built-in AI available and locked while you tune individual feature flags below.
profiles.wizard_ai_posture_mixed_copy: Start from managed AI availability and disable adjacent visual search until reviewed.
profiles.wizard_ai_controls_body: Use the current Firefox 151 AI controls to lock a default posture, tune individual AI features, and keep adjacent browser surfaces deliberate.
profiles.wizard_ai_controls_active: This profile already manages AI behavior. Review the feature-by-feature availability rules, generative AI controls, and visual search before export.
profiles.wizard_ai_feature_controls_body: Start here when you want one default posture with specific overrides for translations, PDF alt text, smart tab groups, link previews, sidebar chatbot, or smart window.
profiles.wizard_ai_adjacent_controls_bod: Use these controls when the profile needs generative AI settings or visual search alongside the AI controls policy.
profiles.wizard_ai_generative_controls_b: Use these fields for the generative AI policy settings exposed by the current Firefox release schema.
profiles.wizard_ai_surfaces_body: Use this area for adjacent browser surfaces that feel AI-adjacent to users, such as visual search entry points.
profiles.wizard_ai_fine_tuning_body: Open fine tuning for adjacent smart browser surfaces such as visual search.
profiles.wizard_ai_section_state_empty: Firefox keeps its usual AI feature availability in this area.
profiles.wizard_ai_governance_body: Use this checklist when built-in AI availability and adjacent smart browser surfaces should stay deliberate and reviewable.
profiles.wizard_ai_governance_active: This profile already has managed AI decisions. Check that built-in AI availability and smart browser surfaces still match the posture you want.
profiles.wizard_ai_surfaces_state_empty: Firefox keeps its usual smart browser surfaces in this area.
profiles.wizard_privacy_vpn_body: Use this area when the browser should not surface Mozilla's built-in VPN workflow to end users. This maps to Firefox IP protection policy controls.
profiles.wizard_privacy_vpn_section_stat: Firefox keeps its usual built-in VPN availability.
profiles.wizard_website_behavior_body: Use this area when Firefox should block sites or open links and files in a managed way.
profiles.wizard_website_access_decision_: Choose the browsing posture first, then decide whether file and protocol handlers need managed routing.
profiles.wizard_website_filter_preset_de: Leave site blocking and allowlist behavior untouched.
profiles.wizard_website_filter_preset_mi: Use this when the profile needs both blocked sites and explicit allowlist exceptions.
profiles.wizard_website_filter_shared_pr: Use this when kiosks or shared stations should stay inside a small approved set of sites.
Use this when the device can browse more broadly, but a few domains must still stay blocked.
profiles.wizard_website_handlers_preset_: Leave file and protocol handlers untouched.
Use this when managed file types or MIME actions matter most.
Use this for mailto, custom URI schemes, and other managed link routes.
Use this when the profile needs both managed file actions and managed protocol routing.
profiles.wizard_website_fine_tuning_body: Open fine tuning when you need blocked-site rules, allowlists, or custom link and file handlers.
profiles.wizard_website_section_state_em: Firefox will open sites, links, and files with its usual behavior until you add managed rules here.
profiles.wizard_website_governance_body: Bring blocked sites, allowed exceptions, file and link handlers, and intranet-style routing into one managed access posture.
profiles.wizard_website_governance_activ: This profile already has a managed site-access posture. Check that blocked sites, handlers, and intranet routing still match the browsing environment you want.
profiles.wizard_website_governance_hando: Open the technical path when this profile needs deeper intranet-only or low-level routing rules beyond the main guided access controls.
profiles.wizard_website_governance_item_: Managed site allow/block rules are already configured here: {count}.
Decide which destinations should be blocked, allowed by exception, or limited to managed sites.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if site access does not need central guardrails.
Managed handler mappings are already configured here: {count}.
Choose whether this profile needs managed file actions, protocol routing, or both.
Leave handlers at Firefox defaults if links and files can open normally.
Single-word address bar entries are already routed toward the intranet path here.
Optional: open deeper routing rules if single-word intranet navigation matters for this environment.
Come back to intranet routing only if this environment needs single-word address bar entries to resolve internally.
profiles.wizard_bookmarks_focus_body: Keep bookmark editing in the advanced layer unless this profile already has managed links or folders to review.
profiles.wizard_bookmarks_section_state_: Firefox will keep its usual bookmarks until you add managed links or folders.
profiles.wizard_settings_search_hint: Search by task, Firefox Settings area, browser behavior, or preset.
profiles.wizard_settings_search_empty: No matching settings found in the wizard.
profiles.wizard_preferences_general_body: Add structured Preferences entries for Startup, Tabs, Downloads, Language, Applications, Network Settings, and Updates.
profiles.wizard_preferences_general_focu: Start from the kind of managed preference work you need here: startup behavior, downloads, everyday browser feel, or a manual key-by-key pass.
Jump to startup presets such as restoring the last session or opening the homepage at launch.
Jump to download-folder and save-location presets.
Jump to smooth scrolling, spellcheck, and similar day-to-day browser behavior preferences.
Jump straight to known General preference keys when this needs a lower-level pass.
profiles.wizard_preferences_home_body: Add structured Preferences entries for Homepage, new tabs, and Firefox Home content surfaces.
profiles.wizard_preferences_search_body: Add structured Preferences entries for the Search panel, suggestions, address bar, and shortcut-like surfaces.
profiles.wizard_preferences_privacy_body: Add structured Preferences entries for tracking protection, passwords, autofill, permissions, HTTPS-Only Mode, DNS over HTTPS, and security controls.
profiles.wizard_preferences_sync_body: Add structured Preferences entries for Firefox Account surfaces and for deciding which data types sync between devices.
profiles.wizard_preferences_presets_body: Start from curated entries, then adjust the raw preference key, value, status, and type when needed.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundles_body: Apply a small, coherent group of related managed preferences at once.
profiles.wizard_preferences_known_body: Add a known key with its suggested type and, when safe, a recommended managed default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_empty: Firefox is using its normal preference behavior here. Add a managed preference only when this section needs a rule that should stay fixed.
profiles.wizard_privacy_review_body: Quick check of privacy, cleanup, site permissions, and cookie behavior before you export this profile.
profiles.wizard_schema_permissions_intro: Pick the permission areas Firefox should manage, then add allow or block lists only for the categories that matter.
profiles.wizard_schema_permissions_group: Camera, microphone, and screen sharing controls usually matter first in managed environments.
Decide whether websites can ask for location and notification access, or whether Firefox should block that flow.
Use these controls for autoplay and VR-style capabilities that may need tighter defaults.
profiles.wizard_schema_permissions_categ: Set the default behavior for this permission area first, then add site-specific allow and block rules only if you need them.
Choose whether Firefox blocks new requests, locks this permission, or uses a built-in default.
List the sites that should always be allowed or blocked for this permission area.
profiles.wizard_schema_authentication_ho: Add one host or origin per row.
profiles.wizard_schema_sites_list_hint: Add one site or origin per row.
profiles.wizard_schema_certificates_inst: Add one certificate file path per row.
profiles.wizard_schema_list_empty_entrie: No entries added yet. Add one when Firefox needs a managed value here.
profiles.wizard_schema_list_empty_hosts: No hosts added yet. Add one when Firefox should trust or treat a host differently here.
profiles.wizard_schema_list_empty_sites: No sites added yet. Add one when Firefox should handle a specific site differently here.
profiles.wizard_schema_list_empty_files: No files added yet. Add one when Firefox should trust a certificate file here.
profiles.wizard_schema_cookies_intro: Choose the default cookie posture first, then add site-specific exceptions only where users need them.
profiles.wizard_schema_cookies_group_def: Set the baseline for normal and private browsing, then lock it only if people should not change it.
profiles.wizard_schema_cookies_group_sit: Allow, session-only allow, or block cookies for specific sites.
profiles.wizard_network_review_title: What Firefox will use for network access
profiles.wizard_network_review_body: Quick check of enterprise sign-in, certificate trust, secure DNS, and Windows sign-in behavior.
profiles.wizard_privacy_section_state_em: Firefox is keeping its normal privacy and password behavior here. Change this area when you need stricter defaults for browsing data, sign-in prompts, or saved credentials.
profiles.wizard_lockdown_section_state_e: Firefox still allows its normal advanced surfaces here. Tighten this area for shared devices, kiosk setups, or locked-down environments.
profiles.wizard_privacy_site_section_sta: Firefox is using its normal site permission and cookie behavior here. Manage this area when specific site categories should be blocked, allowed, or locked.
profiles.wizard_network_enterprise_body: Keep browser networking, certificate trust, single sign-on, and managed DNS controls together in one place.
profiles.wizard_network_enterprise_hint: Choose the common enterprise posture first, then fine-tune trusted hosts and certificate files only if your environment needs more than the baseline.
profiles.wizard_trust_auth_workflow_body: Use this checklist when Firefox should trust internal resources or sign people into corporate services without making every certificate and host rule visible at once.
profiles.wizard_trust_auth_workflow_acti: This profile already carries some enterprise trust or sign-in decisions. Use the checklist below to see whether Windows sign-in, trusted hosts, enterprise roots, and certificate files still need to be aligned.
profiles.wizard_trust_auth_workflow_sign: Windows sign-in or browser-wide enterprise sign-in posture is already managed.
Decide whether this profile should use Windows sign-in or lock browser-wide sign-in behavior for managed access.
Leave this alone unless Firefox should reuse the current Windows identity or lock enterprise sign-in behavior.
profiles.wizard_trust_auth_workflow_host: Trusted host or protocol rules are already configured here: {count}.
Add only the internal hosts or origins that should use SPNEGO, NTLM, delegated auth, or related exceptions.
Use this only when a few internal hosts need managed enterprise sign-in methods.
profiles.wizard_trust_auth_workflow_root: Firefox is already set to trust enterprise roots for this profile.
Choose whether Firefox should inherit enterprise root trust before you start importing certificate files manually.
Turn this on only if Firefox should trust your organization’s existing root store.
profiles.wizard_trust_auth_workflow_file: Certificate files already queued for direct import: {count}.
Add certificate files only if this profile needs trust material that is not already covered by enterprise roots.
Skip this unless Firefox should import specific certificate files directly.
profiles.wizard_network_enterprise_prese: Leave Windows SSO and enterprise roots at normal Firefox behavior.
Turn on managed Windows single sign-on without changing certificate trust yet.
Trust enterprise root certificates distributed by the operating system.
Enable both Windows SSO and enterprise roots as the usual corporate baseline.
profiles.wizard_network_enterprise_fine_: Open this only when host-level authentication rules or direct certificate imports are needed.
profiles.wizard_network_enterprise_secti: Firefox is using its normal enterprise-access behavior here. Add rules in this area when corporate authentication, certificates, or trusted internal access need management.
Windows SSO and enterprise roots enabled
profiles.wizard_schema_authentication_in: Start with browser-wide sign-in behavior, then add trusted hosts only where they are really needed.
profiles.wizard_schema_authentication_gr: Decide whether Firefox locks these rules and whether enterprise sign-in works in private windows.
List only the hosts or origins that should use enterprise sign-in methods.
Use these only when short hostnames or proxy auth exceptions are required in your environment.
profiles.wizard_schema_certificates_intr: Choose whether Firefox should trust enterprise roots first, then add certificate files only if you manage your own trust chain.
profiles.wizard_schema_certificates_grou: This is the fast path when your operating system already distributes the right root certificates.
Add certificate files only when Firefox should import specific trust material directly.
profiles.wizard_schema_handlers_mimeType: Add the MIME types Firefox should handle in a managed way, then choose what the browser should do with them.
profiles.wizard_schema_handlers_schemes_: Add URI schemes such as mailto only when Firefox should route them in a managed way.
profiles.wizard_schema_handlers_extensio: Add file extensions only when Firefox should open them with a managed action or helper app.
profiles.wizard_schema_handlers_helper_a: List helper apps only when Firefox should offer or use specific applications for this rule.
profiles.wizard_home_review_title: What users will see at startup
profiles.wizard_home_review_body: Quick check of homepage behavior, startup pages, Firefox Home, and browser recommendations.
profiles.wizard_search_review_body: Quick check of the default engine, custom engines, built-in engines you hide, and Firefox Suggest.
profiles.wizard_homepage_section_state_e: Firefox will open its normal homepage. Change this when people should start on an internal portal, support page, dashboard, or another managed destination.
profiles.wizard_home_overrides_section_s: Firefox will keep its normal New Tab and post-update pages. Add overrides here when those moments should lead people to managed internal pages instead.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_section_sta: Firefox will keep its normal Home cards and recommendations. Adjust this when the default feed feels too noisy, distracting, or not appropriate for the device.
profiles.wizard_search_defaults_section_: Firefox will keep its normal search setup. Change this when a team should default to one search engine or when built-in choices should be hidden.
Search suggestions are off while Firefox Suggest surfaces stay enabled
profiles.wizard_search_surfaces_workflow: Use this checklist when search should feel managed end to end, from the default engine to custom engines and Firefox Suggest surfaces.
This profile already has some managed search decisions. Check that default engines, engine restrictions, and Firefox Suggest behavior still line up with the browser experience you want.
A managed default engine or basic search posture is already configured here.
Choose whether the browser should default to one managed engine or change the normal search posture.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if search can stay on the normal browser setup.
Custom engines, hidden built-ins, or install restrictions are already configured here.
Decide whether this browser should add custom engines, hide built-ins, or restrict new engine installs.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if people can manage built-in and custom search engines on their own.
Firefox Suggest surfaces are already being managed here.
Choose whether suggestions, sponsored results, and related Firefox Suggest surfaces should stay open, private, or locked down.
Leave this at Firefox defaults if Firefox Suggest does not need central control.
This profile already combines managed defaults with Firefox Suggest rules into one coordinated search experience.
Optional: tighten the search flow further if search defaults and suggestion surfaces should be governed together.
Come back here later if search should become a more coordinated managed experience.
profiles.wizard_search_focus_hint: Start from the search task itself: set a managed default, add custom engines, or tighten what people can install and see.
profiles.wizard_search_defaults_preset_d: Leave normal search behavior untouched for now.
profiles.wizard_search_defaults_preset_m: Use this when the main task is sending everyone to one preferred search engine.
profiles.wizard_search_defaults_preset_c: Use this when Firefox should expose internal or managed search engines alongside built-ins.
profiles.wizard_search_defaults_preset_r: Use this when built-in engines should be hidden or new web-page installs should be blocked.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_section_: Firefox will keep its normal suggestion surfaces. Adjust this when address-bar recommendations should be reduced, expanded, or aligned with privacy expectations.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_focus_hi: Start from the user experience you want in the address bar: leave it open, reduce it for privacy, or lock it down more strictly.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_preset_d: Keep the usual address-bar suggestion surfaces for now.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_preset_p: Use this when the main goal is reducing suggestion surfaces and data-sharing in the address bar.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_preset_m: Use this when recommendation surfaces should stay available but be reviewed and tuned deliberately.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_preset_l: Use this when suggestion surfaces should be turned off and kept locked for the profile.
profiles.wizard_preferences_summary_defa: Set the preference key, value, status, and optional explicit type.
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_default: Use true/false, numbers, or text. JSON objects and arrays are also accepted when value type is inferred.
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_clear: Clear removes the managed value, so no explicit value is needed.
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_known: Known preference with a suggested managed default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_known_m: Known preference with multiple preset variants. Set status and value explicitly.
profiles.wizard_preferences_error_value: Preference value is required unless status is clear.
profiles.wizard_preferences_error_boolea: Boolean values must be true or false.
profiles.wizard_preferences_error_number: Number values must be valid numeric input.
profiles.wizard_preferences_warning_requ: Preference key is required. Value is required unless status is clear.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_resto: Uses browser.startup.page = 3 so Firefox reopens the last session.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_open_: Uses browser.startup.page = 1 to open the configured homepage at launch.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_warn_: Keeps the standard confirmation when a user closes many open tabs.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_last_: Keep window open after the last tab
Turns off closing the whole window when the final tab is closed.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_downl: Keeps downloads in the configured folder instead of prompting every time.
Always ask where to save files
Forces Firefox to ask for a download location each time.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_smoot: Turns smooth scrolling on in the General panel.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_spell: Enables spellcheck in multi-line text areas such as comment forms.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_home_: Enable Firefox Home on new tabs
Keeps the built-in Firefox Home experience available on new tabs.
Displays the top sites strip in Firefox Home.
Shows recent and bookmarked highlights on Firefox Home.
Turns off Pocket stories on Firefox Home.
Removes sponsored tiles from Firefox Home.
Turns off small promotional snippets on Firefox Home.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundle_home_: Keeps search on, but removes stories, sponsored tiles, and snippets from Firefox Home.
Shows Top Sites with two rows and no sponsored tiles.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_searc: Allows provider suggestions in the search field and address bar.
Shows bookmark matches in address bar suggestions.
Shows history matches in address bar suggestions.
Suggest open tabs in address bar
Shows already-open tab matches while the user types in the address bar.
Suggest top sites in address bar
Keeps top-site suggestions visible in the address bar results.
profiles.wizard_preferences_known_home_t: Controls how many rows of top sites Firefox Home shows.
profiles.wizard_preferences_known_search: Shows Firefox quick actions such as clear history or open downloads while typing.
profiles.wizard_preferences_known_privac: Sets the default behavior for sites that want to send desktop notifications.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_searc: Lets single-word address bar entries fall back to search.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_priva: Sends the Do Not Track signal with browsing requests.
Turns off built-in password saving through Preferences.
Stops Firefox from storing and suggesting form history.
Forces Firefox to upgrade navigations to HTTPS where possible.
Sets network.trr.mode = 2 for managed DNS over HTTPS.
Denies geolocation permission prompts by default.
Denies camera permission prompts by default.
Denies microphone permission prompts by default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundle_permi: Blocks location, camera, microphone, and notification prompts by default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundle_doh_c: Switches Firefox to TRR-only mode and keeps enterprise roots available.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_sync_: Keeps bookmark sync enabled for signed-in devices.
Keeps browsing history sync enabled for signed-in devices.
Keeps open-tab sync enabled across devices.
Disables password sync even when Firefox Accounts are available.
profiles.wizard_homepage_body: Configure the initial Firefox landing experience and lock it when the profile should stay consistent.
profiles.wizard_homepage_focus_hint: Start from the landing experience people should get: normal Firefox, a company portal, restored sessions, or a locked homepage.
profiles.wizard_homepage_preset_defaults: Leave startup and homepage behavior at the normal Firefox experience.
profiles.wizard_homepage_preset_portal_c: Use this when people should land on an intranet, dashboard, support page, or another managed destination.
profiles.wizard_homepage_preset_session_: Use this when the main goal is resuming previous work instead of sending everyone to one homepage.
profiles.wizard_homepage_preset_locked_c: Use this when the profile should enforce one managed landing page and keep it fixed.
profiles.wizard_homepage_shared_hint: Shared-device recommendations when the same startup choice needs a tighter posture.
profiles.wizard_homepage_shared_preset_p: Best when the browser should always open on one managed landing page for kiosks, labs, or front-desk devices.
profiles.wizard_homepage_shared_preset_s: Best when the device is shared but should reopen the working session instead of forcing one portal every time.
profiles.wizard_homepage_additional_hint: One URL per line. Firefox will open these alongside the main homepage.
profiles.wizard_homepage_locked_copy: Prevents users from changing the homepage configuration in Firefox.
profiles.wizard_search_body: Set the search bar layout, suggestions, and the built-in engines your users should see first.
profiles.wizard_search_defaults_fine_tun: Open fine tuning when you need to change search bar layout, hide built-in engines, or add managed search engines.
profiles.wizard_search_remove_hint: One engine name per line. Firefox will hide those built-in engines from the search UI.
profiles.wizard_search_add_body: Add the search engines people should see in Firefox, and fill in each card with the details Firefox needs to use them.
profiles.wizard_search_presets_menu_body: Start blank or open preset engines when you want a ready-to-edit template.
profiles.wizard_search_presets_menu_hint: Choose a preset only when it matches the kind of engine you need.
profiles.wizard_search_preset_ticket_cop: Starts a managed search engine for helpdesk or issue tracking.
profiles.wizard_search_preset_wiki_copy: Good starting point for an internal wiki or knowledge base.
profiles.wizard_search_preset_ddg_copy: Ready-to-edit public web search preset with a simple GET query.
profiles.wizard_search_add_empty: No managed search engines yet. Add one when Firefox should offer an internal or custom engine alongside its built-in list.
profiles.wizard_search_engine_card_meta: Adds one search engine for people to use in Firefox.
profiles.wizard_search_engine_summary_de: GET by default. Add the required fields to complete this engine.
profiles.wizard_search_engine_warning_re: Required for a valid engine: Name and Search URL template.
profiles.wizard_search_engine_advanced_b: Open only when this engine needs a custom method, icon, encoding, suggestions URL, description, or POST payload.
profiles.wizard_newtab_body: Tune the new tab page and the extra pages Firefox shows on first run or after an upgrade.
profiles.wizard_home_overrides_focus_hin: Start from the surface you need to manage: new tabs, first-run pages, or a broader managed landing flow.
profiles.wizard_home_overrides_preset_de: Leave new tabs and special first-run or post-update pages untouched.
profiles.wizard_home_overrides_preset_ne: Use this when the main task is enabling or disabling Firefox Home on new tabs.
profiles.wizard_home_overrides_preset_fi: Use this when the profile should send people to a managed page on first run or after updates.
profiles.wizard_home_overrides_preset_ma: Use this when the profile needs both new-tab control and managed first-run or post-update pages.
profiles.wizard_override_first_run_hint: Mozilla allows multiple URLs here, separated with a vertical bar.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_body: Choose which discovery cards appear on the built-in Firefox Home experience.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_focus_hint: Start from the kind of Home experience you want people to land on: default feed, search and shortcuts, a quieter focused page, or a more managed card mix.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_preset_defa: Keep the ordinary Firefox Home feed for now.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_preset_shor: Use this when people should keep search and top sites visible without leaning on content recommendations.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_preset_focu: Use this when the goal is a quieter start page with fewer distractions and recommendation surfaces.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_preset_mana: Use this when the profile needs a more deliberate combination of cards, sponsored surfaces, and layout locking.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_fine_tuning: Open fine tuning for stories, sponsored content, snippets, and card layout.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_recommendat: Open when recommendations, highlights, or sponsored Home surfaces need explicit control.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_snippets_gr: Open when Firefox Home snippets should be shown or hidden explicitly.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_layout_grou: Open when the Home layout must be locked or lower-level Home policies are needed.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_body: Control the recommendation surfaces that Firefox can show in the address bar.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_fine_tun: Open fine tuning for sponsored results, locked state, and the feedback loop behind Firefox Suggest.
profiles.wizard_proxy_body: Set the connection mode first, then fill only the fields that matter for that mode.
profiles.wizard_proxy_preset_hint: Start with the routing model first. After that, add endpoints, PAC URL, bypass rules, or lock state only if this profile needs them.
profiles.wizard_proxy_preset_defaults_co: Leave proxy behavior untouched and let Firefox use its normal routing.
profiles.wizard_proxy_preset_none_copy: Force Firefox to connect without any proxy.
profiles.wizard_proxy_preset_system_copy: Use the operating system proxy settings already managed on the device.
profiles.wizard_proxy_preset_pac_copy: Start a managed PAC-based setup, then add the PAC URL below.
profiles.wizard_proxy_preset_manual_copy: Start a manual proxy setup, then fill in the proxy hosts and bypass rules below.
profiles.wizard_proxy_section_state_empt: Firefox is using its normal proxy behavior. Set this only when traffic must follow a managed proxy, PAC file, or system rule.
profiles.wizard_proxy_section_state_none: Firefox connects directly without a proxy.
profiles.wizard_proxy_section_state_syst: Firefox follows the operating system proxy settings.
profiles.wizard_proxy_section_state_auto: Firefox will try automatic proxy discovery (WPAD).
Add a PAC URL to finish auto-config setup.
profiles.wizard_proxy_section_state_manu: Add at least one manual proxy endpoint.
profiles.wizard_proxy_manual_hint: Fill only the protocols you actually route through the proxy. Leave the rest empty.
profiles.wizard_proxy_auto_config_hint: Paste the PAC URL and Firefox will use it for proxy decisions.
profiles.wizard_proxy_passthrough_hint: Enter one hostname per line or comma-separated. Firefox will connect to these directly instead of using the proxy.
profiles.wizard_proxy_locked_copy: Prevents users from changing proxy configuration in Firefox.
profiles.wizard_proxy_use_http_all_label: Use HTTP proxy for all protocols
profiles.wizard_proxy_use_http_all_copy: Applies the HTTP proxy to SSL, FTP, and SOCKS traffic where appropriate.
profiles.wizard_proxy_auto_login_copy: Lets Firefox automatically authenticate to the proxy with the current user context.
profiles.wizard_proxy_use_dns_copy: Routes DNS through the proxy when SOCKS v5 is in use.
profiles.wizard_extensions_body: Choose the default add-on posture first, then add the install URLs and add-on IDs you want Firefox to manage.
profiles.wizard_extensions_rollout_hint: Start with the rollout model you want. Then use the fields below only for the add-ons that need managed URLs, lock state, or per-add-on exceptions.
profiles.wizard_extensions_preset_defaul: Leave the shared add-on posture alone and only add rules below when a specific extension needs one.
profiles.wizard_extensions_preset_open_c: Allow general add-on installs while keeping managed rollout rules optional.
profiles.wizard_extensions_preset_blocke: Block add-on installs by default and only allow exceptions where you add them explicitly.
profiles.wizard_extensions_preset_manage: Block installs by default, then add the managed install URLs and known add-on rules this profile should carry.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_se: Choose the extension governance model once. It sets the default install posture and opens the right details only when needed.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_bl: Block add-on installs unless a later rule explicitly allows them.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_ma: Block installs by default and open install URL or ID rules for the allowed set.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_cu: Block installs by default and open known add-on cards for managed rollout choices.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_mi: Use both curated add-on cards and raw install URL or ID rules.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_bo: Use this checklist to confirm the shared install posture, the managed rollout set, and whether this profile still needs deeper extension policy exceptions.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_ac: This profile already carries extension governance decisions. Use the checklist below to see whether the shared posture, rollout rules, and deeper exceptions are all covered.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_ha: Open deeper extension rules only when this profile needs install-source exceptions or arbitrary per-add-on policy branches.
profiles.wizard_extensions_governance_it: Choose the shared add-on posture first so Firefox knows whether unapproved installs stay open or blocked by default.
Shared add-on posture is already set to {value}.
Add managed install URLs or known add-on rules if this profile should carry an approved rollout set.
Managed rollout add-on entries already covered: {count}.
No deeper extension exceptions yet. That is fine unless this profile needs install-source allowlists or arbitrary ExtensionSettings rules.
Install-source or arbitrary add-on policy entries already configured: {count}.
profiles.wizard_extensions_review_body: Quick check of recommended add-ons, extra add-on rules, and custom install sources.
profiles.wizard_bookmarks_review_title: How managed bookmarks are set up
profiles.wizard_bookmarks_review_body: Quick check of bookmark links, managed folders, and nested bookmark trees.
profiles.wizard_website_access_review_ti: How websites and links will behave
profiles.wizard_website_access_review_bo: Quick check of blocked sites, allowed exceptions, and file or link handlers.
profiles.wizard_extensions_profiles_body: Keep the install mode visible up front, then open details only when a known add-on needs its own download link or extra browser behavior.
profiles.wizard_extensions_profiles_empt: Known add-ons are still following the shared default rule. Add a card only when one add-on needs its own install, lock, or uninstall behavior.
profiles.wizard_extension_profile_update: Keeps this extension pinned unless you update it explicitly.
profiles.wizard_extension_profile_privat: Sets whether this extension is enabled in Private Browsing windows.
profiles.wizard_website_filter_state_emp: Sites will open normally until you add blocked sites or exceptions
profiles.wizard_permissions_state_empty: Firefox will keep site permissions at their usual defaults until you choose categories to manage.
profiles.wizard_permissions_nested_state: This permission follows Firefox defaults until you add allowed or blocked sites
profiles.wizard_cookies_state_empty: Firefox will keep its usual cookie rules until you add site exceptions or a stricter default.
profiles.wizard_handlers_state_empty: Firefox will use its usual file and link handlers until you add managed mappings
profiles.wizard_handlers_nested_dictiona: No entries yet. Add one when this file or link type needs a managed rule
profiles.wizard_handlers_nested_object_e: No handler rule yet. Add an action or helper app when Firefox should open this in a managed way
profiles.wizard_handlers_nested_array_em: No helper apps yet. Add one if Firefox should hand this type off to a specific app
profiles.wizard_user_messaging_state_emp: Firefox will keep its usual onboarding and recommendation surfaces until you change them here
profiles.wizard_extensions_mode_hint: Sets what Firefox should do with add-ons that do not have their own rule.
profiles.wizard_extensions_fine_tuning_b: Open fine tuning to manage install URLs and locked add-ons without leaving the guided flow.
profiles.wizard_extensions_section_state: Firefox will keep its usual add-on behavior until you choose a managed install policy here.
Known add-ons with their own rules: {count}
profiles.wizard_extensions_summary_body: Install URLs add managed add-ons. Locked IDs keep an add-on installed. Uninstall IDs remove known add-ons from the profile.
profiles.wizard_extensions_install_hint: One URL or file path per line. Firefox will install these add-ons for users.
profiles.wizard_extensions_locked_hint: One add-on ID per line. Firefox will keep these add-ons installed and harder to remove.
profiles.wizard_extensions_uninstall_hin: One add-on ID per line. Firefox will remove these add-ons from managed browsers.
profiles.wizard_extensions_advanced_body: Use the full ExtensionSettings and install-permission policies for arbitrary add-on IDs, install sources, blocked messages, and other advanced fields. These controls stay in sync with the simpler extension fields above.
profiles.wizard_export_section_ready_bod: Save, validate, and download from one compact handoff area.
profiles.wizard_export_section_changes_b: Check the profile outcome by area, then confirm CIS exceptions and the handoff summary.
profiles.wizard_export_section_technical: Open compatibility, schema boundary, and raw-policy details only when you need the technical view.
profiles.wizard_export_review_body: These items can still go into the saved files, but they deserve one more explicit look before you pass the profile on.
profiles.wizard_export_validation_pendin: Optional: run one more validation check before download.
profiles.wizard_export_validation_invali: Fix this profile before the final download step.
profiles.wizard_export_state_unsaved_exi: Save the latest edits before downloading.
profiles.wizard_export_state_invalid_dir: Fix the current profile file before saving. Download links still point to the last saved version.
profiles.wizard_export_state_archived: Archived profiles cannot be exported until restored.
profiles.wizard_export_ready_create: Save this profile first, then come back here to download Firefox policies.json.
profiles.wizard_export_ready_saved: This saved version is ready for Firefox policies.json download.
profiles.wizard_export_compatibility_bod: Use this technical review to inspect advanced-only items, older keys, or unknown keys.
profiles.wizard_export_boundary_register: What still stays advanced on purpose
Some areas remain in All settings deliberately so the wizard stays task-first instead of turning into a schema mirror.
Low-level preference bundles still stay in All settings until they form a clearer scenario-first workflow.
TLS, cipher, and niche security internals stay technical because shallow presets here would be easy to misread.
Deep AI policy objects stay in All settings when they are too detailed for the guided posture step.
Open-ended nested objects still stay advanced when they are too irregular for a stable guided form.
profiles.wizard_export_drilldown_body: Use this tighter drill-down when you want to understand the advanced-only, deprecated, or unknown tail before opening the full JSON editor.
profiles.wizard_export_drilldown_raw_bod: This item is preserved in the saved files, but it still lives outside the main guided flow on purpose.
profiles.wizard_export_drilldown_depreca: This is an older key. It may still work for your environment, but it deserves a quick handoff review.
profiles.wizard_export_drilldown_unknown: This key is outside the current schema catalog. Verify it before you hand the profile off.
profiles.wizard_export_drilldown_empty: No advanced-only, deprecated, or unknown tail needs a closer drill-down right now.
profiles.wizard_cis_review_recommendatio: No CIS recommendation id is attached.
profiles.wizard_export_next_steps_clear: Nothing is blocking download right now.
profiles.wizard_export_ready_now_empty: This checklist will fill in as the profile takes shape.
profiles.wizard_export_included_title: Included in the policies.json you download now
profiles.wizard_export_included_body: This explains what the Firefox policies.json download will package right now.
profiles.wizard_export_included_saved_la: The policies.json download uses the latest saved version.
profiles.wizard_export_included_saved_pr: The policies.json download still uses the last saved version.
profiles.wizard_export_included_counts: The saved policies.json currently contains {policies} top-level policies and {preferences} managed preferences.
profiles.wizard_export_included_raw: Advanced-only items preserved in the saved policies.json: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_included_deprecat: Deprecated policy settings included in the saved policies.json: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_included_unknown: Unknown top-level keys included in the saved policies.json: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_included_cis: {level} contributed applied CIS decisions before your final edits: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_included_empty: Save the profile first to create a downloadable policies.json.
profiles.wizard_export_missing_title: Not in the downloaded policies.json yet
profiles.wizard_export_missing_body: These are the changes or states that the current policies.json download does not include yet.
profiles.wizard_export_missing_unsaved_n: This draft is not in any downloadable policies.json yet.
profiles.wizard_export_missing_unsaved_e: Your current unsaved edits are not in the downloaded policies.json yet.
profiles.wizard_export_missing_invalid: The current broken document is not used for policies.json download until it is fixed and saved.
profiles.wizard_export_missing_archived: Archived profiles stay unavailable for download until restored.
profiles.wizard_export_missing_empty: Nothing is waiting outside the downloaded policies.json right now.
profiles.wizard_export_review_raw: Check advanced-only items so the next person knows why they stay outside Guided editor: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_review_deprecated: Review deprecated settings before handing the profile off: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_review_unknown: Review unknown top-level keys before handing the profile off: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_review_cis_manual: Review CIS conflicts where the base scenario stayed in place: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_review_empty: Nothing else stands out for technical review right now.
profiles.wizard_export_baseline_summary_: The chosen starting point still sets the tone for this profile.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_restore_prof: Restore this archived profile before exporting anything.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_save_first: Create the profile record first so policies.json has a saved version to use.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_save_latest: Save the latest edits so the downloaded policies.json matches what you see here.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_fix_current_: Fix the current profile file before the final download step.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_validate_onc: Run one more validation pass before you download.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_review_raw: Review advanced-only items before handing this profile off: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_review_depre: Review deprecated policy settings before final export: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_plan_review_unkno: Review unknown top-level keys before final export: {count}.
profiles.wizard_export_download_hint_uns: The policies.json download stays disabled until this profile is saved for the first time.
The policies.json download still uses the last saved version until you save these edits.
profiles.wizard_export_download_hint_inv: The current file needs fixes. The policies.json download still points to the last saved version.
profiles.wizard_export_download_hint_arc: Restore this archived profile to enable policies.json download again.
profiles.wizard_export_download_hint_rea: The policies.json download already uses the latest saved version.
profiles.wizard_lifecycle_summary_body: Latest known lifecycle markers for this profile or draft.
profiles.wizard_export_ready_body: Download the saved Firefox policies.json. If you still need a technical pass, open All settings.
profiles.wizard_export_guided_summary_bo: Review the six-step outcome in one place, then jump back into any area that still needs tuning.
profiles.wizard_export_guided_empty: Firefox defaults stay in place here. Change this area only if the profile needs a managed rule.
profiles.wizard_export_shareable_body: Use this plain-language handoff when someone needs the profile outcome without reading the full policies.json or step-by-step review.
profiles.wizard_export_shareable_status_: This handoff text updates as the profile changes.
Could not copy the summary. You can still select the text manually.
profiles.wizard_export_shareable_notice_: Firefox mostly stays on its normal defaults in the guided areas.
profiles.wizard_export_guided_features_a: Known add-ons with their own rules: {count}
profiles.wizard_step_actions_idle: Use these step tools when you want to back out of local changes without resetting the whole profile.
profiles.wizard_step_actions_clean: This step still matches the current profile state.
profiles.wizard_step_actions_both: This step has local changes. You can undo the current pass or reset the step to the saved profile baseline.
profiles.wizard_step_actions_undo_only: This step changed during the current pass. You can undo those local edits.
profiles.wizard_step_actions_reset_only: This step differs from the saved profile baseline. Reset it if you want to start this area over.
profiles.wizard_step_actions_invalid: Fix the current policies.json before undoing or resetting this step.
profiles.wizard_step_actions_read_only: This review step does not make profile changes, so there is nothing to undo or reset here.
profiles.wizard_step_reset_done: Step reset to the current profile baseline.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_empty: As you move through the wizard, the latest changed steps will stay visible here.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_active: The latest changed steps stay here so you do not have to jump to export just to remember what moved.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_step_setup: Updated setup details, scenario choice, or starting baseline.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_step_browser: Changed browser access, startup, search, or navigation defaults.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_step_privacy: Changed privacy, hardening, site permissions, or cleanup posture.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_step_feature: Changed add-ons, websites, languages, sync, or managed browser surfaces.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_step_ai: Changed built-in AI availability or smart browser surfaces.
profiles.wizard_step_memory_step_generic: Changed guided settings on this step.
profiles.wizard_finish_name_required: Profile name is required before finishing.
profiles.wizard_starter_applied_with_cis: Starter baseline and CIS overlay applied.
profiles.wizard_network_applied: Landing, search, and proxy settings updated.
